spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates that cross-site iframe access to a blob URL is blocked, but the spec change keys blob URLs by (top-level site, origin, has-cross-site-ancestor) — the demo simplifies to a single same-vs-cross-site lane and never surfaces the tripartite key.

interactive.rationale: Real createObjectURL, real fetch from a srcdoc iframe with postMessage round-trip — actually exercises the partition.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Index blurb promises blob URLs keyed to top-level site with cross-site fetches failing; the page delivers that.

edge_cases.rationale: Missing: revoked-then-fetched case, blob URL passed through window.open / a.target=_blank navigation carve-out, third-party iframe loaded via http vs srcdoc (the spec distinguishes these).

references.rationale: The see-also section now links to the correct ChromeStatus feature 5130361898795008.

design_system.rationale: All variables, no raw hex in page-level CSS.

openQuestions.title: Surface the full storage key
openQuestions.detail: Spec keys on (top-level site, origin, has-cross-site-ancestor). The demo currently reports `top-level site` only. Print the full triple so authors understand the partitioning rule.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate the top-level navigation carve-out
openQuestions.detail: The spec keeps top-level navigations partitioned only by frame origin — i.e. window.open(blobUrl) from a different site can still navigate. Add a button that opens the blob URL in a new tab to show the carve-out.

summary: Real working probe of the cross-site iframe block, but it still under-reports the full storage key and lacks the top-level navigation carve-out.
